concepts.name: Deque
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure that supports insertion and deletion at both ends.

concepts.name: Abstract Data Type
concepts.short_definition: A mathematical model for data structures, defining operations without specifying implementation.

concepts.name: Linear Data Structure
concepts.short_definition: A data structure where elements are arranged in a sequential manner.

concepts.name: Queue
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure following FIFO principle for operations.

concepts.name: Stack
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure following LIFO principle for operations.

concepts.name: Insertion
concepts.short_definition: The operation of adding an element to a data structure.

concepts.name: Deletion
concepts.short_definition: The operation of removing an element from a data structure.

concepts.name: Front
concepts.short_definition: The beginning of a linear data structure.

concepts.name: Rear
concepts.short_definition: The end of a linear data structure.

concepts.name: Add Front
concepts.short_definition: Adds an element to the front of the deque.

concepts.name: Add Rear
concepts.short_definition: Adds an element to the rear of the deque.

concepts.name: Remove Front
concepts.short_definition: Removes and returns the element from the front of the deque.

concepts.name: Remove Rear
concepts.short_definition: Removes and returns the element from the rear of the deque.

concepts.name: Peek Front
concepts.short_definition: Returns the element at the front of the deque without removing it.

concepts.name: Peek Rear
concepts.short_definition: Returns the element at the rear of the deque without removing it.

concepts.name: Is Empty
concepts.short_definition: Checks if the deque contains any elements.

concepts.name: Size
concepts.short_definition: Returns the number of elements in the deque.

concepts.name: Array Implementation
concepts.short_definition: Implementing a deque using an array.

concepts.name: Linked List Implementation
concepts.short_definition: Implementing a deque using a linked list.

concepts.name: Doubly Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linked list where each node has pointers to both the next and previous nodes.

concepts.name: Circular Array
concepts.short_definition: An array that uses a single, fixed-size buffer as if it were connected end-to-end.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: Measures the efficiency of an algorithm in terms of the input size.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: Measures the amount of memory an algorithm uses.

concepts.name: Amortized Analysis
concepts.short_definition: A way to analyze the average performance of an operation over a sequence of operations.

concepts.name: Array-based Add Front Complexity
concepts.short_definition: Time complexity of adding an element to the front of an array-based deque.

concepts.name: Array-based Add Rear Complexity
concepts.short_definition: Time complexity of adding an element to the rear of an array-based deque.

concepts.name: Array-based Remove Front Complexity
concepts.short_definition: Time complexity of removing an element from the front of an array-based deque.

concepts.name: Array-based Remove Rear Complexity
concepts.short_definition: Time complexity of removing an element from the rear of an array-based deque.

concepts.name: Linked List-based Add Front Complexity
concepts.short_definition: Time complexity of adding an element to the front of a linked list-based deque.

concepts.name: Linked List-based Add Rear Complexity
concepts.short_definition: Time complexity of adding an element to the rear of a linked list-based deque.

concepts.name: Linked List-based Remove Front Complexity
concepts.short_definition: Time complexity of removing an element from the front of a linked list-based deque.

concepts.name: Linked List-based Remove Rear Complexity
concepts.short_definition: Time complexity of removing an element from the rear of a linked list-based deque.

concepts.name: Applications of Deque
concepts.short_definition: Real-world scenarios where deques are utilized.

concepts.name: Palindrome Check
concepts.short_definition: Using a deque to efficiently check if a string is a palindrome.

concepts.name: Sliding Window Problems
concepts.short_definition: Using deques to solve problems involving a window of elements that slides over a sequence.

concepts.name: Browser History
concepts.short_definition: Simulating back and forward navigation using deques.

concepts.name: Task Scheduling
concepts.short_definition: Managing tasks with priorities or deadlines using deques.

concepts.name: Double-ended Priority Queue
concepts.short_definition: A variation of a priority queue that allows efficient retrieval of both minimum and maximum elements.

concepts.name: Generic Programming
concepts.short_definition: Writing code that can work with different data types without modification.
